name: shipment_tracking
description: Unattended batch parcel-status check. Reads a text file of tracking numbers (one per line), looks each up against the carrier API deterministically, classifies an outcome per parcel (delivered / out_for_delivery / in_transit / delayed / escalate / not_found / error), and writes a single dated results file with a per-outcome summary. Never asks the user anything.

variables.description: Path to the plain-text file holding one tracking number per line. Default: examples/shipment_tracking/tracking-ids.txt. To process a different batch, point the decide_parcels step's itemSource.file at another file.
The list of decided records, one per tracking number, collected by the decide_parcels listDecision step.

settings.action: For each tracking number, look up its live status from the carrier API and classify an outcome.

itemVariables.description: The carrier tracking number for this parcel.
true if the carrier lookup failed, timed out, or returned a non-200 / empty response.
true if the carrier says the tracking number is not found / invalid.
The carrier status text, e.g. delivered, out for delivery, in transit, exception, returned, lost.
The parcel's last known location reported by the carrier.
The carrier's estimated delivery date (ISO YYYY-MM-DD), if any.
true if the parcel is in transit and its estimated delivery is more than the 7-day threshold away.
true when the outcome needs human attention (delayed or escalate).
A short human-readable note describing the parcel's status.

settings.action: Read the decided records in the `tracking_results` slot. Build a results object with a `summary` block at the top — `total` (the number of records) and a `counts` map giving how many records fall under each `outcome` value — followed by `results` (the full list of records, each with tracking_number, outcome, status, last_location, estimated_delivery, note, and needs_attention where set). Write this object as pretty JSON to a file named `tracking-status-<today>.json` in the current working directory, where <today> is today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g. tracking-status-2026-06-22.json). Do not ask any questions; this is an unattended batch run.
